transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas
SOURCE: Associated Press

ARTICLE:

Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles Thousands of people protesting mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump marched in Southern California on Sunday. Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked a major freeway. Protests were also held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas. CORRECTS NAME OF THE FREEWAY - Law enforcement personnel stage in front of a group of demonstrators who shut down the 101 freeway during a protest calling for immigration reform Sunday, Feb. 2, 2025, in Los Angeles.
```json
{
  "summary": "Thousands of demonstrators protested against mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump across multiple locations on Sunday, February 2, 2025. In downtown Los Angeles, protesters shut down the 101 freeway, bringing major disruption to traffic in the area. Law enforcement personnel staged responses to the freeway blockade. The demonstrations called for immigration reform and featured protesters holding signs, waving flags including Mexican flags, and chanting slogans. Some demonstrators spray-painted walls along the 110 freeway and painted signs on vehicle windows. The protests also gathered on overpasses over the 110 freeway. Similar protests occurred simultaneously in Texas, with demonstrations taking place in Dallas and McAllen. The coordinated protests represented a significant show of opposition to the Trump administration's planned deportation policies, with participants expressing their concerns through direct action by blocking major transportation infrastructure.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Thousands of protesters marched against President Trump's planned mass deportations on Sunday, February 2, 2025",
    "Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked the 101 freeway, causing major traffic disruption",
    "Law enforcement personnel staged responses to the freeway shutdown and protesters",
    "Protests also occurred in Dallas and McAllen, Texas on the same day",
    "Demonstrators held signs, waved flags, shouted slogans, and spray-painted walls calling for immigration reform"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Associated Press"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: Thousands of demonstrators protested against mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump across multiple locations on Sunday, February 2, 2025. In downtown Los Angeles, protesters shut down the 101 freeway, bringing major disruption to traffic in the area. Law enforcement personnel staged responses to the freeway blockade. The demonstrations called for immigration reform and featured protesters holding signs, waving flags including Mexican flags, and chanting slogans. Some demonstrators spray-painted walls along the 110 freeway and painted signs on vehicle windows. The protests also gathered on overpasses over the 110 freeway. Similar protests occurred simultaneously in Texas, with demonstrations taking place in Dallas and McAllen. The coordinated protests represented a significant show of opposition to the Trump administration's planned deportation policies, with participants expressing their concerns through direct action by blocking major transportation infrastructure.
key_facts: Thousands of protesters marched against President Trump's planned mass deportations on Sunday, February 2, 2025
Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked the 101 freeway, causing major traffic disruption
Law enforcement personnel staged responses to the freeway shutdown and protesters
Protests also occurred in Dallas and McAllen, Texas on the same day
Demonstrators held signs, waved flags, shouted slogans, and spray-painted walls calling for immigration reform
